title: Toasted Mexican Couscous Rajas Con Crema
description: A kid-friendly Mexican dish featuring toasted couscous, lardons, and a creamy sauce, served in a traditional talavera plate.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit to roast the poblano peppers.
Render the lardons in a skillet over medium heat until crispy, then set aside.
Toast the couscous in a dry skillet over medium heat, stirring frequently, until lightly browned.
Sauté the diced onion and minced garlic in a skillet over medium heat until the onion is translucent.
Add the roasted and diced poblano peppers to the skillet and stir to combine.
In a large bowl, combine the toasted couscous, sautéed onion and pepper mixture, and crispy lardons.
In a small bowl, whisk together the crema, queso fresco, chopped cilantro, and lime juice.
Pour the crema mixture over the couscous mixture and stir to combine.
Season with salt to taste, then serve in a traditional talavera plate.
